verbs.name: help
verbs.description: Print the manifest-derived list of verbs as JSON; intended for SessionStart / SessionEnd reason=compact hooks.

result_fields.name: verbs
result_fields.description: Array of {name, description} for every verb in the manifest.

verbs.name: spawn
verbs.description: Launch a tracked Claude Code instance inside a new tmux session. Fire-and-forget: returns the claude_instance_id; state moves from pending to waiting on the first SessionStart hook.

params.name: cwd
params.description: Absolute (or ~/-prefixed) path the Spawn's Claude starts in. Required.

params.name: template
params.description: Optional named template under ~/.agent-director/templates/. Per-call params layer on top per SRD §7.1 (scalars replace; maps merge; permissions arrays concat; claude_args replaces wholesale).

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Optional explicit id (UUID4 minted when absent). Collision against a live row returns ErrInstanceIdCollision.

params.name: label
params.description: Repeated KEY=VALUE pairs. Each becomes AGENT_DIRECTOR_LABEL_<UPPER_KEY> on the session env and persists in labels.

params.name: allow
params.description: Repeated permissions.allow entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.

params.name: deny
params.description: Repeated permissions.deny entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.

params.name: ask
params.description: Repeated permissions.ask entries concatenated with the user / project tiers.

params.name: relay-mode
params.description: on / off. Empty falls back to config defaults.relay_mode (default off).

params.name: extra-env
params.description: Repeated KEY=VALUE pairs injected on the tmux session env. Reserved keys (AGENT_DIRECTOR_*) rejected; auth env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) allowed.

params.name: claude_args
params.description: Pass-through argv to `claude` after the supervisor's own flags. Denied: --settings, --resume, --continue, --print, --output-format.

params.name: no-pre-trust
params.description: Skip pre-writing projects.<cwd>.hasTrustDialogAccepted=true into the spawn's .claude.json (resolves to <CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR>/.claude.json if CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set in extra-env, otherwise ~/.claude.json). Default off (pre-trust IS performed so Claude Code skips its workspace-trust dialog and the Spawn becomes interactive immediately).

params.name: tmux-session-name
params.description: Optional explicit tmux session name. Empty/omitted falls back to <basename(cwd)>-<id[:8]>. Validated app-side: rejects empty (when supplied), '#' ':' '.', ASCII control chars, non-UTF-8, and >64 bytes. NO DB uniqueness check; live-collision surfaces as the wrapped tmux new-session error. Name reuse across ended spawns is supported.

result_fields.name: claude_instance_id
result_fields.description: The id (caller-supplied or freshly-minted UUID4) the row is tracked under.

verbs.name: status
verbs.description: Return the current state of a tracked Spawn (pending/waiting/working/ask_user/check_permission/ended/missing).

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn to inspect.

result_fields.name: state
result_fields.description: Current state column value.

verbs.name: get
verbs.description: Return the full DB row for a tracked Spawn (id, parent, state, cwd, session name, args, relay mode, session_id, labels, timestamps).

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn to fetch.

result_fields.name: claude_instance_id
result_fields.description: Stable id of the Spawn.

result_fields.name: parent_id
result_fields.description: Parent Spawn id (AGENT_DIRECTOR_INSTANCE_ID env at spawn time), empty when launched by a human shell.

result_fields.name: state
result_fields.description: Current state column value.

result_fields.name: cwd
result_fields.description: Canonicalized cwd.

result_fields.name: tmux_session_name
result_fields.description: tmux session under which the Spawn is running.

result_fields.name: claude_args
result_fields.description: Verbatim argv passed through to claude after --settings.

result_fields.name: relay_mode
result_fields.description: on / off.

result_fields.name: jsonl_path
result_fields.description: Last known transcript path. Empty until a future Epic persists it; resume composes the path on demand from cwd + claude_session_id.

result_fields.name: claude_session_id
result_fields.description: Claude Code session UUID, extracted from SessionStart hook's transcript_path.

result_fields.name: labels
result_fields.description: Caller-supplied labels.

result_fields.name: started_at
result_fields.description: Row insert time.

result_fields.name: last_seen_at
result_fields.description: Last hook UPSERT time.

result_fields.name: ended_at
result_fields.description: Set when state moves to ended (omitted while live).

result_fields.name: permission_requests
result_fields.description: All open (undecided) permission requests awaiting orchestrator decision. Always a non-null array ([] when empty). Populated only when state == check_permission; empty array for all other states. Each element: request_id (int) — autoincrement row id; request_token (string) — UUIDv4 token minted by runRelay, pass to decide verb to target this row; tool_name (string) — Claude Code tool that triggered the request; tool_input (string) — raw JSON string of the tool's input, NOT a nested object (consumers parse it themselves); requested_at (RFC3339 timestamp) — created_at of the row.

verbs.name: send-keys
verbs.description: Send text into a tracked Spawn's tmux pane. `\r` bytes are stripped (prevent premature submission); `\n` bytes are preserved (composed-but-unsubmitted newlines in Claude's input box); a single Enter is always appended to submit the composed buffer.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the live Spawn to drive.

params.name: text
params.description: Text to type into the Spawn's input. `\r` stripped pre-send; `\n` preserved as newline-in-input.

params.name: allow_pending
params.description: When true, also permit send-keys on a pending Spawn (pre-SessionStart use case). ended/missing Spawns are still rejected even with this flag set.

verbs.name: read-pane
verbs.description: Capture the last N lines of a tracked Spawn's tmux pane. Default 25 lines, no upper cap. Default ANSI handling strips escape codes but preserves unicode TUI glyphs (❯, ⎿, 🐝). `ansi=true` returns raw bytes.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn to read.

params.name: n_lines
params.description: Number of trailing pane lines to return. Defaults to 25 when 0/omitted. No upper cap.

params.name: ansi
params.description: When true, return raw bytes from tmux (escape codes preserved). When false (default), strip ANSI sequences while preserving unicode glyphs.

params.name: allow_pending
params.description: Accepted for surface symmetry with send-keys. ReadPane has no state guard (pending/ended/missing are all readable), so this flag has no behavioral effect.

result_fields.name: pane
result_fields.description: Captured pane text. ANSI handling depends on the `ansi` parameter.

verbs.name: kill
verbs.description: Terminate the Spawn's tmux session. Idempotent on terminal states (ended/missing). Does NOT promise state cleanup — the row stays in its prior state until find-missing reconciles it.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn to kill.

verbs.name: decide
verbs.description: Orchestrator's allow/deny verdict on an open PermissionRequest. Race-free first-call-wins via a single-statement UPDATE guarded by `decision IS NULL`. Only callable on Spawns with relay_mode=on.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn sitting on the PermissionRequest.

params.name: request_token
params.description: UUIDv4 token identifying the specific permission request to decide. Minted by runRelay per-request; required to enforce per-row isolation when multiple concurrent requests exist for the same Spawn.

params.name: decision
params.description: Either `allow` or `deny`.

params.name: reason
params.description: Currently discarded on deny; the canonical DecisionReasonOperator is persisted regardless. Reserved for future schema additions.

verbs.name: resume
verbs.description: Bring a terminated (ended/missing) Spawn back to life via `claude --resume`. Same claude_instance_id, fresh tmux session, same JSONL transcript. parent_id is re-derived from the caller's AGENT_DIRECTOR_INSTANCE_ID env var on every resume.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the terminated Spawn to resurrect.

result_fields.name: claude_instance_id
result_fields.description: The same id passed in (resume preserves the instance id across resurrection).

verbs.name: find-missing
verbs.description: Reconcile DB state against live processes. Scans live-state rows (including pending), diffs against the OS probe (Linux /proc / macOS sysctl), transitions unprobeable rows to `missing`. Degraded-mode guard: 0 readable processes + ≥1 live rows → log warning + refuse to write.

result_fields.name: count
result_fields.description: Number of rows transitioned to missing on this sweep. Zero is a legitimate happy-path result when nothing needed reaping (or when the degraded-mode guard refused to write).

result_fields.name: ids
result_fields.description: Sorted IDs of rows transitioned to missing.

verbs.name: expire
verbs.description: Remove terminal-state rows (ended/missing) whose ended_at is older than the retention window. Default window is config defaults.expire_retention_days; --older-than overrides. Does NOT touch tmux or JSONL transcripts.

params.name: older_than
params.description: Duration override (e.g. `7d`, `2h`, `0d`). When omitted, defaults.expire_retention_days from config applies.

result_fields.name: count
result_fields.description: Number of rows removed. Zero is a legitimate happy-path result when no terminal rows matched the retention window.

result_fields.name: ids
result_fields.description: Sorted IDs of rows removed.

verbs.name: delete
verbs.description: Admin batch removal by claude_instance_id. Bypasses all guards. Does NOT touch tmux sessions or JSONL transcripts. Per-row result map records ok/error per id; the batch never aborts on a partial failure.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id(s) to delete. Repeatable on CLI; JSON array via MCP.

result_fields.name: results
result_fields.description: Per-id result: "ok" on success, an err_name string on failure.

verbs.name: make-template
verbs.description: Save a reusable spawn preset. The TOML file lands under ~/.agent-director/templates/<name>.toml; spawn --template <name> applies it. Reserved per-invocation params (template, claude_instance_id, tmux_session_name) are NOT accepted.

params.name: name
params.description: Template name. Must be filename-safe (no path separators, no leading dot, no `..`).

params.name: cwd
params.description: Bake a default cwd into the template. Per-call --cwd overrides.

params.name: relay_mode
params.description: Bake a default relay_mode (on/off). Per-call --relay-mode overrides.

params.name: claude_args
params.description: Bake default Claude argv. Per-call --claude-args REPLACES the template's array wholesale (not concat).

params.name: extra_env
params.description: Bake env-var entries. Per-call --extra-env merges by key; per-call wins on collision.

params.name: label
params.description: Bake label k=v entries. Per-call --label merges by key; per-call wins on collision.

params.name: allow
params.description: Bake permissions.allow entries. Per-call --allow CONCATENATES (does not replace).

params.name: deny
params.description: Bake permissions.deny entries. Per-call --deny CONCATENATES.

params.name: ask
params.description: Bake permissions.ask entries. Per-call --ask CONCATENATES.

params.name: overwrite
params.description: Replace any existing template at this name atomically. Default false preserves O_EXCL create-only semantics.

result_fields.name: path
result_fields.description: Absolute path of the written template file.

verbs.name: list
verbs.description: Enumerate Spawn rows. All filters AND together. Returned order is unspecified — callers sort with jq etc.

params.name: state
params.description: Filter by state. Multiple values OR together. Comma-separated on CLI; JSON array via MCP.

params.name: label
params.description: Filter by label k=v. Repeatable on CLI; each entry must contain a literal `=`. Multiple entries AND together.

params.name: parent
params.description: Filter by parent_id exact match.

params.name: cwd
params.description: Filter by canonicalized cwd exact match.

params.name: tmux-session-name
params.description: Filter by tmux session name exact match. Returns any live or ended row whose tmux_session_name equals the value byte-for-byte; correlation across re-uses, not uniqueness enforcement.

params.name: limit
params.description: Cap result count. 0 / omitted means no cap.

result_fields.name: spawns
result_fields.description: Matching rows. Empty array when none match (never null).

verbs.name: pause
verbs.description: Politely shut down a waiting Spawn by sending `/exit` and waiting up to pause.timeout_seconds for the row to reach `ended`. One-shot — no caller-side polling. Terminal states (ended/missing) are no-op success.

params.name: claude_instance_id
params.description: Id of the Spawn to pause.

verbs.name: serve
verbs.description: Start the stdio MCP server. Long-lived process that exposes every other verb as an MCP tool over JSON-RPC on stdin/stdout. Typically registered with `claude mcp add agent-director <binary-path> serve --stdio`.

params.name: stdio
params.description: Enter the stdio MCP loop (required for v1; other transports may land in future Epics).

verbs.name: version
verbs.description: Print the binary's build-time version stamp as JSON ({version, commit}). Used by install.sh to verify a local binary matches the current source tree before installing it.

result_fields.name: version
result_fields.description: Human-readable version stamp from `git describe --tags --always --dirty` at build time. "dev" for unstamped builds.

result_fields.name: commit
result_fields.description: Full git SHA the binary was built from. "unknown" for unstamped builds.

verbs.name: hook
verbs.description: Internal: invoked by Claude Code on lifecycle events via the per-Spawn --settings hooks. Reads payload JSON from stdin, writes a row UPSERT, exits 0 (state-tracking fail-open).

params.name: stdin
params.description: Claude Code hook payload (hook_event_name, transcript_path, tool_name, reason, ...).
